objective: Implement the fifth #298 child slice: reconcile registry-dependent governance surfaces and add drift validation.
scope_boundary: Reconcile README and STANDARDS repo-list surfaces with docs/governed_repos.json.
Reconcile explicit GitHub Actions checkout surfaces that cannot be dynamically generated.
Reconcile raw-feed, graphify helper, sweep, metrics, Codex, compendium, and memory selection paths with registry or manifest sources.
Add validator coverage so duplicate repo lists are registry-driven, validator-backed, or explicitly deferred.
Add issue-backed planning, execution-scope, exception, review, progress/backlog, and validation evidence.
out_of_scope: Enabling EmailAssistant subsystems before EmailAssistant#1 resolves repo-local bootstrap.
Final org-wide e2e closeout, which remains #314.
research_summary: After #311 and #312, the governed repo registry covers active org repos. The weekly sweep already derives sweep/code_governance/graphify/metrics/compendium/memory selections from registry or manifests, but explicit actions/checkout blocks, raw-feed sync, local graphify symlink preparation, README text, and STANDARDS repo summaries still contained static or stale repo-list surfaces. This slice adds seek-and-ponder explicit checkout where required, derives raw-feed and local graphify helper selections from registry/manifest data, updates README/STANDARDS, and adds scripts/overlord/validate_registry_surfaces.py plus local/CI wiring.

sprints.name: Surface Reconciliation
sprints.goal: Remove or guard static repo-list drift points.
tasks: Add seek-and-ponder checkout blocks to sweep and nightly cleanup workflows.
Drive raw-feed sync from raw_feed_sync subsystem flags.
Drive local graphify symlink preparation from scheduled graphify targets.
Update README and STANDARDS repo registry text.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: duplicate repo lists are registry-driven, validator-backed, or explicitly static due GitHub Actions checkout mechanics.
Final AC: README/STANDARDS cannot silently drift from registry coverage.

sprints.name: Validator Coverage
tasks: Wire validator into graphify-governance-contract and Local CI Gate.
Validate graphify target reconciliation and subsystem selections.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: graphify target reconciliation passes.
Final AC: sweep/metrics/compendium/raw-feed/memory/Codex selection tests pass for expanded registry entries.
Final AC: branch protection/default branch/ruleset/required-check validation remains issue-backed by #311/#312 evidence and downstream blockers.

sprints.name: Final E2E Proof
sprints.goal: Run the focused #298 registry-dependent command matrix before merge.
tasks: Run registry, graphify, and registry-surface validators.
Run structured plan, execution-scope, backlog sync, workflow contract, py_compile, bash syntax, diff hygiene, and Local CI Gate.
Run read-only specialist review before PR publication.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: Local CI Gate passes for changed paths.
Final AC: #313 remains open unless PR and e2e evidence are linked.
Final AC: parent #298 remains open until #314 complete.

specialist_reviews.focus: Static repo-list surfaces, workflow checkout alignment, validator coverage, and #313 acceptance criteria.
specialist_reviews.summary: Read-only review found the validator was duplicating registry subsystem membership and README retained a fixed workflow count. Both follow-ups were applied before validation.

alternate_model_review.summary: Subagent review accepted the surface reconciliation after removal of duplicate validator membership state and README count cleanup.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #313 may edit governance repository workflow/document/helper/validator/planning surfaces. It may not edit downstream repos or enable EmailAssistant subsystems.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Complete validators, run final e2e matrix, apply review follow-ups, then open PR for #313.

material_deviation_rules: If downstream repo edits are needed, stop and create repo-local issue-backed work.
If EmailAssistant subsystem enablement is needed, stop and leave it blocked behind EmailAssistant#1.
If final epic closeout is requested, stop and leave it to #314.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules, no HITL, e2e testing as final AC
